title: Fragrant Gooey Turkish Peanuts Manti
description: A traditional Turkish dish featuring peanuts, glaze, and a hint of pepper, made in a sattvic-friendly way, this manti is a nostalgic treat. It is a perfect snack for any time of day.

ingredients.name: glaze
ingredients.notes: made from grape juice and cornstarch

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, water, and sesame oil to make the dough.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into small pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a thin circle.
Place a few peanuts in the center of each circle.
Fold the dough over the peanuts to form a triangle or square shape, and press the edges together to seal the manti.
Heat a large skillet over medium heat and add a small amount of sesame oil.
Cook the manti for 5 minutes on each side, until they are golden brown.
While the manti are cooking, prepare the glaze by heating the grape juice and cornstarch in a small saucepan over low heat.
Once the manti are done, brush them with the glaze and sprinkle with black pepper, turmeric, and chopped scallions.
